texto: To learn the way of the Buddha is to learn about oneself. To learn about oneself is to forget oneself. To forget oneself is to be enlightened by everythingin the world. To be enlightened by everything in the world is to let fall one's own body and mind.
explicacao: Citação de Dogen (1200-1253) sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “To learn the way of the Buddha is to learn about oneself. To learn about oneself is to forget one…” articula reflexao, philosophy-of-life, oneself em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: To learn the way of the Buddha is to learn about oneself. To learn about oneself is to forget oneself. To forget oneself is to be enlightened by everythingin the world. To be enlightened by everything in the world is to let fall one's own body and mind.
